chunks.text: College of Engineering Department of Electrical Engineering & Computer Sciences Instructional & Electronics Support Group January 11, 1996
January 11, 1996 On-line access to AMS MathSciNet -------------------------------- The following is a reprint of an email message from Catherine Candee, Librarian at UC Berkeley. Qu
m Catherine Candee, Librarian at UC Berkeley. Questions to ccandee@library.berkeley.edu please. EECS Instructional & Electronics Support 384/386 Cory Hall, 333 Soda Hall ins

33 Soda Hall inst@eecs.berkeley.edu ============================================================================= From ccandee@library.berkeley.edu Wed Jan 10 14:05:31 1996 From: Catherine Candee Subject: On-line ac

31 1996 From: Catherine Candee Subject: On-line access to Math journals available We now have unlimited access to MathSciNet, the AMS's online version of Mathematical Reviews, from any Berkeley IP address or the home IP address. No password or user

dress or the home IP address. No password or user ID is required. This subscription entitles us to: a. make searches of MathSciNet b. make one or more copies in hard copy form of the output of any search; such copies may not be sold and may n

ny search; such copies may not be sold and may not be distributed to anyone who is not an authorized user; and c. download search results to hard disk or diskette, provided that such data is not made available to anyone who is not an author

ot made available to anyone who is not an authorized user. According to the terms of our contract, an authorized user may not make MathSciNet available to anyone other than another authorized user, whether by telephone link or by permitting access

whether by telephone link or by permitting access through his or her terminal or computer, or by other similar or dissimilar means or arrangements. If you are unclear about the scope of our license or would like to see a copy of our subscription

or would like to see a copy of our subscription agreement, please see me in the library. If you haven't already, you can find MathSciNet at: http://www.ams.org/mathscinet/ I have a supply of AMS's "Quick Start Card", a 5.5" x 8.5" (very brief)

S's "Quick Start Card", a 5.5" x 8.5" (very brief) instruction guide for getting started on MathSciNet. Send me e-mail if you would like one, or stop by the library to pick one up. Thanks for you patience while we ironed out the last hundred wrinkl

tience while we ironed out the last hundred wrinkles. Catherine Candee, Librarian Astronomy/Mathematics/Statistics Library =============================================================================
